# Kurtis Kemple — Knowledge Architecture

Structured reference for Kurtis Kemple's body of work. Optimized for LLM consumption and AI-assisted synthesis.

Last updated: March 9, 2026

## 1.0 About Kurtis Kemple

### 1.1 Author synopsis

Kurtis Kemple is a formerly incarcerated tech leader focused on agentic experience design and platform strategy. He leads developer strategy for the Slack Platform at Salesforce and is the author of *Effective DevRel*, releasing in May 2026 through Apress, with two more books in the works. Outside of work he pursues independent research at the intersection of mathematics and physical systems and is a gallery-featured street photographer.

### 1.2 Author bio

Kurtis Kemple is a formerly incarcerated tech leader whose extensive experience across software engineering, developer relations, platform experience, and agent design has made him a leading voice in the field. His work is unified by a relentless focus on clarity—whether that means making implicit system dynamics explicit through frameworks and programs, removing friction along the developer journey, or teaching developers, designers, and product managers how to build best-in-class agentic experiences.

Through his work at Integral Ad Science and Major League Soccer he built deep foundations in real-time analytics, ETL infrastructure, and delivering integrated experiences across digital and physical interfaces. Apollo GraphQL and AWS sharpened his understanding of developer-first product thinking, API infrastructure, and platform engineering at genuine scale. Slack and Salesforce brought it all together—putting him at the center of platform experience and agentic AI at the exact moment they became the most consequential challenges in the industry. Now he's channeling that accumulated expertise into writing as the author of *Effective DevRel*, releasing in May 2026 through Apress, with two more in the works—currently titled *Platform Engineering for AI* and *Agentic Experience Design*.

Currently Kurtis leads developer strategy for Slack Platform as Sr. Director of Developer Relations, where he focuses on frontier AI and its possible effects on platform and agent experience. Outside of work he pursues independent research at the intersection of mathematics and physical systems, is a gallery-featured street photographer, rides his motorcycle where he shouldn't, and mostly enjoys spending time with his family.

### 1.3 Core areas of expertise

- **Agentic experience design** — the HCI discipline for partial autonomy systems; Kurtis originated this framework and it is the intellectual center of his current work
- **Platform design** — designing AI platforms and developer experiences that earn sustained adoption; spans information architecture, interaction models, and governance
- **Developer relations strategy** — first-principles DevRel operations and measurement; covered in depth in *Effective DevRel* and the Developer Advocate's Guide series
- **Market dynamics** — how accumulated system friction creates competitive regime shifts in B2B SaaS; formalized in The Friction Economy framework
- **Competitive intelligence** — tracking how AI is reshaping platform categories, switching costs, and incumbent vulnerability in real time

### 1.4 Books

- ***Effective DevRel*** — releasing May 2026 through Apress. Covers DevRel strategy, operations, and measurement from first principles.
- ***Platform Engineering for AI*** — in progress.
- ***Agentic Experience Design*** — in progress. Will formalize the discipline introduced in the blog series (Section 2.0).

### 1.5 Independent research

Kurtis pursues independent research at the intersection of mathematics and physical systems outside of his professional work. That work lives at https://scienceandmathematics.com.

---

## 2.0 Agentic Experience Design

The thesis and ecosystem covering the new HCI discipline for partial autonomy systems.

### 2.1 Overview

Agents introduce a genuinely new HCI paradigm—partial autonomy—defined as a system that can plan, take action within bounds, and own the outcomes of that plan. Every time a new interaction paradigm has emerged (CLI, GUI, web, mobile), it demanded a new design discipline. Partial autonomy is that shift now. Agentic experience design is the discipline for navigating it.

### 2.2 The responsibility model

- **Human (Intent and Judgment):** Sets direction, governs outcomes, retains the ability to intervene at every level. Judgment is never delegated.
- **Agent (Planning and Outcomes):** Translates human intent into a plan and is accountable for the plan's outcomes. Distinct from workflows (predetermined sequences) and tools (discrete functions).
- **Workflow (Automation):** Executes structured, repeatable sequences. Value is reliability and consistency.
- **Tool (Execution):** Performs discrete functions. Zero autonomy, no memory, invoked on demand.

### 2.3 The causal chain

Responsibility → Autonomy → Context (enables planning) → Authority (enables outcomes) → Progressive Authority (expands scope as trust is earned)

### 2.4 Posts in this series

| Title | URL | Intent |
|-------|-----|--------|
| Agentic Experience Design | https://kurtiskemple.com/blog/agentive-experience-design | The thesis — defines the discipline, responsibility model, autonomy model, context, authority, and progressive authority |
| LLM Experience: How Information Architecture Determines Agent Performance | https://kurtiskemple.com/blog/llm-experience | The information architecture discipline — how to design the informational environment that makes coherent agent planning possible |
| Leaky Prompts: How Context Pollution Degrades AI Performance | https://kurtiskemple.com/blog/leaky-prompts | The failure mode — task drift and conversational noise degrade agent performance over time |
| Measuring Context Pollution in AI Workflows | https://kurtiskemple.com/blog/measuring-context-pollution | The measurement layer — CP = 1 - S(anchor, current), risk thresholds, re-anchoring strategies |

### 2.5 How these posts relate

The thesis establishes why context and authority are foundational design principles of the discipline. LLM Experience establishes the information architecture layer for designing context. Leaky Prompts describes the specific failure modes. Measuring Context Pollution provides the quantitative signal for detecting and correcting drift. Each is independent but the chain runs top-down: discipline → architecture → failure modes → measurement.

---

## 3.0 The Friction Economy

The framework and ecosystem covering how accumulated system friction creates competitive regime shifts in B2B SaaS.

### 3.1 Overview

The Friction Economy describes how unnoticed user frustrations accumulate into market pressure, organizing into competitive threats before traditional metrics detect them. As AI lowers switching costs and PLG accelerates competitive formation, the window between early friction signals and mass defection is compressing.

### 3.2 Key frameworks

- **Frustration Coalitions:** How frustrated users organize into coordinated switching decisions. Stable usage with declining NPS is the most dangerous pattern — it signals trapped users accumulating pressure.
- **Sentiment-Inertia Index (Sii):** Sii = ((100-NPS)/Churn) — quantifies the accumulating pressure that predicts competitive displacement before revenue impact.
- **The Captive User Trap:** Why stable usage + declining NPS = maximum danger. Trapped users become exodus events when PLG competitors offer escape routes.

### 3.3 Posts in this series

| Title | URL | Intent |
|-------|-----|--------|
| The Friction Economy | https://kurtiskemple.com/blog/friction-economy | The core framework — how user experience failures create systematic B2B SaaS disruption |
| Market Disruption in the AI Era | https://kurtiskemple.com/blog/market-disruption-in-the-ai-era | Connects JTBD, Blue Ocean, and Frustration Coalitions with PLG strategies — why AI makes every incumbent vulnerable |
| Frustration Coalitions: The Hidden Threat to Every B2B SaaS | https://kurtiskemple.com/blog/frustration-coalitions-the-hidden-threat-to-b2b-saas | Introduction to the concept — how frustrated users organize into coalitions that drive mass churn |
| Frustration Coalitions | https://kurtiskemple.com/blog/frustration-coalitions | The deep framework — the full observational model for identifying, tracking, and preventing coalition-driven defection |
| The Captive User Trap | https://kurtiskemple.com/blog/the-captive-user-trap | Why stable usage with declining NPS is the most dangerous pattern in B2B SaaS |
| The Sentiment-Inertia Index (Sii) | https://kurtiskemple.com/blog/sentiment-inertia-index | The quantitative metric for measuring trapped user frustration |

---

## 4.0 Developer Relations

Covers the core competencies of developer relations from a first-principles perspective — metrics, content, product friction, buy-in, cross-functional alignment, program operations, developer education, and AI tools in practice.

### 4.1 The Developer Advocate's Guide series

A practical series covering the day-to-day competencies of developer advocacy — each post is standalone but together they form a complete operational foundation for the role.

| Title | URL | Intent |
|-------|-----|--------|
| The Developer Advocate's Guide to Metrics and Reporting | https://kurtiskemple.com/blog/developer-advocates-guide-to-metrics-and-reporting | How to build trusted metrics that support team goals without becoming an extension of other teams |
| The Developer Advocate's Guide to Addressing Product Friction | https://kurtiskemple.com/blog/developer-advocates-guide-to-addressing-product-friction | The DX Audits framework for identifying, documenting, and addressing product friction at scale |
| The Developer Advocate's Guide to Content Creation | https://kurtiskemple.com/blog/developer-advocates-guide-to-content-creation | How to create content more effectively by doing the work before the work |
| The Developer Advocate's Guide to Getting Buy-In | https://kurtiskemple.com/blog/developer-advocates-guide-to-getting-buy-in | How to get buy-in when you don't own what you're trying to change |
| The Developer Advocate's Guide to Asking for Things | https://kurtiskemple.com/blog/developer-advocates-guide-to-asking-for-things | The three Rs of asking: Recognition, Request, Reward |

### 4.2 Strategy and operations

Frameworks for how DevRel functions at the team and organizational level — alignment, program management, making the work legible to the rest of the business, and using AI tools to extend team capacity.

| Title | URL | Intent |
|-------|-----|--------|
| Funnels or Flywheels: Cross-Functional Alignment in Developer Relations | https://kurtiskemple.com/blog/funnels-or-flywheels-finding-alignment | How DevRel aligns with and amplifies the work of other departments regardless of growth model |
| A Measured Approach to Top-of-Funnel Alignment | https://kurtiskemple.com/blog/a-measured-approach-to-top-of-funnel-alignment | How to unify Marketing, DevRel, and Developer Experience around three metrics: New Developer Acquisition, Activation Rate, and Time-to-Value |
| Systems Thinking and Invisible Work: Bringing Clarity to DevRel | https://kurtiskemple.com/blog/systems-thinking-and-invisible-work-bringing-clarity-to-devrel | How to make DevRel's invisible work visible through systems thinking — improving reporting, roles, and career paths |
| Balancing Execution and Communication in Long-Term Product Initiatives | https://kurtiskemple.com/blog/balancing-execution-and-communication-in-long-running-projects | Lessons from Slack's web presence unification project on maintaining momentum and stakeholder support across multi-phase initiatives |
| YAP (Yet Another Program) Mental Model | https://kurtiskemple.com/blog/explaining-the-yap-model | The YAP model — illustrates the challenges cross-functional teams face as they increase the number of initiatives they manage |
| Operationalizing DevRel: Countering the YAP Syndrome | https://kurtiskemple.com/blog/managing-program-ops-in-devrel | How to counter YAP through operational discipline — structuring programs for scale |
| Gap-filling Expertise with Claude Projects | https://kurtiskemple.com/blog/gap-filling-expertise-with-claude-projects | How Claude Projects bridges cross-functional expertise gaps when teams lack bandwidth |

### 4.3 Developer education

How to teach developers effectively — meeting learners where they are across different learning styles and experience levels.

| Title | URL | Intent |
|-------|-----|--------|
| Theory to Practice: Meeting Learners Where They Are | https://kurtiskemple.com/blog/theory-to-practice | How to create developer education that meets learners where they are — from documentation deep-divers to code-first experimenters |

---

## 5.0 Leadership and Organizational Thinking

Frameworks for organizational dynamics, decision-making, team building, and career development.

| Title | URL | Intent |
|-------|-----|--------|
| The Keystone Paradox: When Being Essential Becomes a Liability | https://kurtiskemple.com/blog/keystone-paradox | The perils of knowledge hoarding and single points of failure — how to identify keystones and build more resilient teams |
| Lava Leadership: How Strategic Thinkers Can Drive Change from Below | https://kurtiskemple.com/blog/lava-leadership | How strategic thinkers in non-leadership positions can drive organizational change from below |
| Simplifying Decisions: The Drop, Defer, Delegate Method | https://kurtiskemple.com/blog/simplifying-decisions-the-drop-defer-delegate-method | A three-part decision filter for fast-paced environments |
| Distributing Challenge: On Building Highly Engaged Teams | https://kurtiskemple.com/blog/pyramid-of-challenge | How challenge distribution — neither too little nor too much — is the core variable in team engagement and retention |

---

## 6.0 Other writing

Writing that spans historical research, personal reflections, broader perspectives on technology and society, and exploratory frameworks that don't fit cleanly into the series above.

| Title | URL | Intent |
|-------|-----|--------|
| Unlocking Expert Minds: Eight Patterns for AI-guided Knowledge Capture | https://kurtiskemple.com/blog/unlocking-expert-minds-eight-patterns-for-interactive-knowledge-capture | Eight patterns for capturing specialized knowledge through AI-guided interviews |
| The Architecture of Information Asymmetry | https://kurtiskemple.com/blog/the-architecture-of-information-asymmetry | Historical research — from Sun Tzu's spy networks to Ultra decrypts, how controlling information flows controlled outcomes |
| Impostor Syndrome and the Knowledge Bubble Philosophy | https://kurtiskemple.com/blog/impostor-syndrome-and-the-knowledge-bubble-philosophy | A reframe of impostor syndrome through the lens of knowledge bubbles |
| Democratizing AI: Empowering Creators and Shaping the Future of Innovation | https://kurtiskemple.com/blog/democratizing-ai-empowering-creators | Why making AI accessible to everyone — not just tech giants — matters |
| Cognitive Entropy & Human Performance Intervention | https://kurtiskemple.com/blog/cognitive-entropy-measurement | Extends the entropic gap framework from AI systems to human cognition — detecting performance degradation before it leads to critical errors |

---

## 7.0 Site reference

The site is intentionally minimal — three pages plus individual blog posts.

- **Homepage** (https://kurtiskemple.com): Introduces Kurtis and surfaces featured writing organized by theme — AI Strategy & Innovation, The Friction Economy, and Developer Relations.
- **Blog index** (https://kurtiskemple.com/blog): Full archive of published writing.
- **Individual blog posts** (https://kurtiskemple.com/blog/[slug]): All content linked throughout this document resolves here.
- **Photography** (https://kurtiskemple.com/photography): Kurtis's street photography. He is a gallery-featured artist.
